code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    rsi_period = params['rsi_period']
    atr_period = params['atr_period']
    ma_period = params['ma_period']
    rsi = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_period)
    atr = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=atr_period).average_true_range()
    ma = df['close'].rolling(window=ma_period).mean()
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[(df['close'] > ma) & (rsi < 30) & (df['close'] - df['low'] > atr)] = 1
    signals[(df['close'] < ma) & (rsi > 70) & (df['high'] - df['close'] > atr)] = -1
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/USD exhibits momentum reversal when RSI indicates oversold/overbought conditions, confirmed by ATR-filtered price deviations from the moving average.
